recent_calls.summary: The call was very brief and disconnected immediately after the agent's greeting, with no interaction from the user.

The user, Kate, informed the agent that she was waiting outside for a technician and was up next for the appointment. The agent acknowledged the update and said they would inform the team. The call ended shortly after without further discussion.

The user called to discuss payment for an HVAC repair and mentioned that the money is now in the bank ready for payment. The agent informed the user that they do not have direct access to billing information and transferred the call to the billing team for assistance.

The user, Terry from American Home Shield, called to confirm that the member, Adrian Scheffer, wants a CIL offer instead of a repair for an EC service involving a refrigerant leak at the compressor. The agent confirmed the details, including the correct service address and contact preferences, and assured that the Budget team would reach out to the American Home Shield authorization department as requested. The user expressed positive feedback about the customer service experience.

The user, Steve Santel, called to discuss a recent air conditioner service call and requested to speak with a live agent. The initial agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help.

The user called to cancel an appointment scheduled for April 30th but did not know the time. The agent acknowledged the cancellation request and initiated a transfer to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.

The user called Budget and provided their service address in Edwardsville, Illinois. They requested to speak with a live agent, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called Budget and requested to speak to a representative. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The call was very brief and disconnected almost immediately after the agent's greeting, with no interaction from the user.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer successfully.

The user called to inquire about rescheduling an appointment but was unclear about the time and did not confirm any changes. The agent attempted to assist with rescheduling but the conversation was brief and ended without resolution.

Kate Brown called to inquire about the timing of a service technician appointment she had scheduled, but the agent could not find the appointment in the system. The call was then transferred to a live representative for further assistance.
The call was very brief and disconnected almost immediately after the agent's initial greeting. No meaningful interaction or information exchange occurred.
The user called and requested to speak with Amy. The agent attempted to connect the user and successfully transferred the call to another agent.

The user called to confirm the time frame for an appointment with technician Devon scheduled for tomorrow. The agent provided the appointment window between 10 AM and 11 AM and noted the user's request for a later time, promising to pass it on to the team for follow-up. The call ended with the user declining further assistance.

Isabella called on behalf of her father regarding an AC service scheduled through their home warranty. She inquired about tracking the technician's arrival and provided details about the issue and contact information. The agent confirmed the service details and noted Isabella's request for a callback before 3 PM to update on the technician's timing.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and the user hanging up immediately without any further interaction.
The user received a call from the agent and requested to be connected to a live agent. The agent promptly transferred the call to another agent to assist the user further.

Lisa called to set up a service appointment for her HVAC system through American Home Shield because it does not come on. The agent confirmed the details and scheduled the request for the next day, informing Lisa that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing will contact her soon. Lisa declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.
The call was very brief and ended immediately after the agent greeted the user, Lisa. No conversation or information exchange took place.
The user called and requested to speak with a secretary or receptionist. The agent attempted to transfer the call to a live agent who could assist, and the transfer was successful.

Beverly from American Home Shield called to request a report for an air conditioning work order but needed to verify details with the member before proceeding. The agent offered assistance and invited Beverly to call back when ready. The call ended with no report request completed.
The user returned a call to Budget and spoke briefly with the agent, Sophie, who attempted to assist but transferred the call to another number for further help.

The user, Valerie, called to arrange a furnace modification repair service, specifying that Budget already has the details on file and requesting the service for the next day. The agent confirmed the service details and available time slots, and the user agreed to any time. The call ended after the user expressed some unrelated concerns and pressed digits on the phone.
The call was very brief and disconnected quickly, reaching voicemail without any meaningful interaction.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent promptly transferred the call to another live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to urgently speak with customer service regarding a potential cancellation of an appointment due to no contact or arrival from the service provider. The agent acknowledged the issue and proceeded to transfer the call to another number for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about a quote for an air conditioner repair after a technician had already visited. The agent confirmed the details and arranged for the team to follow up with the user as soon as possible regarding the quote. The user declined to provide feedback and ended the call politely.

The user called to confirm the details of their appointment, which was found to be scheduled for May 7th from 8 to 9 AM with technician Amory. The user then requested to speak to an operator, and the call was successfully transferred. No further issues were reported during the call.
The user called back and the agent attempted to connect them with a live agent by transferring the call. The call was successfully transferred to another number.
The user requested to speak to customer service and the agent transferred the call to a live agent named Gigi. The transfer was successful and the call ended shortly after.
Patricia from American Home Shield called Budget to get assistance on a heating work order. After confirming the issue type, the agent transferred the call to a technician for further help.

Joseph called to inquire about a furnace installation appointment scheduled for 9:30 AM, but the agent could not find any appointment in the system. Joseph requested to be transferred to a live representative, and the call was successfully transferred.

The agent confirmed Andrew Rapp's appointment scheduled for today with technician Patrick between 10 AM and 11 AM. The user acknowledged the appointment but did not specify any changes or additional requests before the call ended.
The user requested to speak with a customer service representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to inquire about the technician's arrival time and was informed that the appointment was scheduled between 8 AM and 9 AM. Since it was already 10 AM, the user requested to flag the issue for follow-up. The agent confirmed the request and assured the user that the team would follow up soon.
The user called and requested to speak with a customer service representative. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Geraldine from American Home Shield called to inquire about lifting the cancellation of a recall for a ductless split air conditioning unit for member Joan Gordon. The agent confirmed the details and assured that Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up soon. The user declined information about maintenance programs and expressed satisfaction with the call.
The call was initiated by Stephanie Stitt but ended shortly after the agent's greeting without any further conversation or assistance provided.
Douglas returned a call and was greeted by Sophie from Budget. Sophie attempted to connect Douglas with a live agent and successfully transferred the call. The call ended shortly after the transfer.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, acknowledged the request and completed the transfer successfully.

Bernita called to schedule a routine air conditioner maintenance check as part of her whole home maintenance program. The agent confirmed her details, including her address and preferred early afternoon appointment time, and assured her that the request would be passed to the team for confirmation. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.

The user, Tanya Taylor, called to inquire about a proposal for repairing or replacing her air conditioner unit under a home warranty plan. The agent determined it was not an emergency and transferred the call to a live representative for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about HVAC service in the Foley area and reported an AC unit issue that could be addressed during a service appointment within the next 24 to 48 hours. The agent confirmed the user's details, corrected the service address, and clarified the home warranty dispatch number. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback.

The user called to schedule a routine air conditioner check for the morning of June 16th but was informed there were no available slots on that day. The user then requested June 17th, but the call was transferred to a live agent before the booking could be completed. The user also specified not wanting a particular technician.

The user, Whitney Davis, called to urgently schedule air conditioning repair service at 5764 Goodfella Boulevard, ZIP code 63136. The agent booked an appointment for the next day at 10 AM after confirming details and the user's role as renter. The user declined to provide an email and declined to give feedback at the end of the call.

Joe Gardner called to report that his air conditioner, covered under American Home Shield, was supposed to be fixed but the technician never came. The agent confirmed details including the service address, issue, and updated contact information to Whitney Davis, and scheduled service for the morning or early afternoon. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback and the agent confirming the service request.

The user called to report a missed air conditioning service at their home in St. Louis, Missouri, and requested a follow-up service either today or Thursday. The agent confirmed the details and attempted to assist but the user indicated they were speaking to the wrong person and requested a direct number for Budget. The call ended with the agent transferring the user to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance without any detailed conversation.

Maggie from American Home Shield called to follow up on a denied work order for a customer's forced air furnace and central air conditioning. The agent confirmed details including the dispatch number and preferred service window within 24 to 48 hours. The call ended with confirmation of the information but no booking was requested.
